title: Mrid
content:
{{Top|leg|notoc=1}}
[[File:Mrid.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Two mrids on Velmor.]]
{{Quote|Centuries past, the Mrid Hunt played a part in our people's survival! Before important functions, the king and specially chosen guests hunted the beasts, in preparation for a royal feast!|A Velmorian herald|<ref name="marvel49">[[Star Wars (1977) 49|''Star Wars'' (1977) 49]]</ref>}}
'''Mrids''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of small, pink, four-legged, rabbit-like creatures that were native to the [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Velmor/Legends|Velmor]]. For centuries, the royal assemblage of Velmor bred the creatures and would engage in a ceremonial act known as a [[Mrid Hunt]]. The hunt harkened back to the years when natives of Velmor were forced to travel as pack hunters just to survive. [[3 ABY/Legends|Three years]] after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and Prince [[Denid]] participated in a Mrid Hunt in the capitol [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Den Velmor]]. They rode astride large [[Ycaqt]]s and broke into small parties to chase the mrids into the surrounding forests.<ref name="marvel49" /> Mrids had highly developed survival instincts and were adept at making themselves difficult to find, making the hunt a challenge.<ref name="cswe">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Mrids first appeared in [[Star Wars (1977) 49|''Star Wars'' (1977) 49]], written by [[Mike W. Barr]] and released in [[1981]]. In the comic, they were illustrated by [[Walter Simonson]].<ref name="marvel49" />
